title: Food Skills & Psychological Resilience Predict Gluten-Free Diet Adherence in Adults with Celiac Disease

summary.hook: This cross-sectional study used an anonymous online survey of adults with self-reported clinician-diagnosed celiac disease (n=327) to examine whether food skills and psychological resilience are associated with gluten-free diet (GFD) adherence.
summary.summary_30s: This cross-sectional study used an anonymous online survey of adults with self-reported clinician-diagnosed celiac disease (n=327) to examine whether food skills and psychological resilience are associated with gluten-free diet (GFD) adherence. Correlational screening identified food skills (meal planning, food preparation, food safety), resilience, and age as variables linked to adherence. In multiple linear regression, meal planning and resilience remained significant explanatory variables, jointly explaining 12.3% of variance in GFD adherence scores, suggesting interventions focused on meal-planning skills and resilience-building may improve adherence.
talktrack_2m: Focus: This cross-sectional study examined the relationship between food skills, psychological resilience, and GFD adherence in adults with celiac disease.
Approach: Population: Adults (≥18 years) with self-reported celiac disease diagnosis. Primary outcome: Gluten-free diet adherence score (instrument not named).
Finding: Correlational analysis identified exploratory variables linked to GFD adherence: food skills (meal planning, food preparation, food safety), resilience, and age.

Why it matters: Authors’ stated conclusions: Food skills and psychological resilience play key roles in gluten-free diet adherence; meal planning strategies and resilience building could be targets for intervention programs to improve wellbeing in individuals with celiac disease. Implications (poster-grounded): Programs that increase meal-planning competence and resilience may support adherence to GFD in adults with celiac disease. Future research should evaluate outcomes and efficacy of these interventions for promoting adherence. Mechanism/explanation (ONLY if stated): Behavioral and psychosocial factors may influence adherence success; specific mechanisms not stated. Connections to prior work (ONLY if referenced): Not stated on poster
key_numbers: Adults (≥18 years) with self-reported celiac disease diagnosis.
Meal planning + resilience explained 12.3% of variance in GFD adherence scores.
Study included 327 adults (≥18) with self-reported doctor-diagnosed celiac disease.
This cross-sectional study used an anonymous online survey of adults with self-reported clinician-diagnosed celiac disease (n=327) to examine whether food skills and psychologic...
In multiple linear regression, meal planning and resilience remained significant explanatory variables, jointly explaining 12.3% of variance in GFD adherence scores, suggesting...
takeaways: Correlational analysis identified exploratory variables linked to GFD adherence: food skills (meal planning, food preparation, food safety), resilience, and age.
Multiple linear regression identified meal planning and resilience as significant explanatory variables for GFD adherence.
The model explained 12.3% of the variance in adherence scores.
limitations: The poster does not list formal study limitations.
The poster leaves open this clarification: What instruments/scales were used to measure GFD adherence, food skills (meal planning/prep/safety), and resilience?

what_to_ask.question: What were the effect sizes for meal planning and resilience—how much does adherence score change per unit increase in each?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This gets the presenter to interpret the main result instead of repeating the title.

what_to_ask.question: Were regression assumptions checked (normality, linearity, heteroscedasticity)?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This probes confidence, assumptions, or limitations without sounding hostile.

what_to_ask.question: What would a meal-planning intervention look like for adults with CD (shopping lists, cross-contamination prevention, recipe planning)?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This opens a useful conversation about why the work matters or where it goes next.
